LanguageData.WVC_Label_fixVanillaGeneImmunityCheck: Fixing and improving vanilla compatibility with mods
LanguageData.WVC_ToolTip_fixVanillaGeneImmunityCheck:
A small set of harmony patches fixing some vanilla game bugs. It almost has no impact on game performance, since everything listed below is rare triggers that work under specific conditions.
1) Fix (replace) the gene check for disease immunity by adding a check for gene activity. Thus, genes that add immunity will not provide immunity when overridden by other genes. 2) Checking the pawn's geneset for errors after generation. Fixes missing abilities. Removes traits left by already removed genes. Corrects override by removing information about non-existent genes. Resets the cache of some genes. 3) Fix for duplicate flag. Pawns that are duplicates will not stop being duplicates after being unspawned and spawned again. The duplicate flag is used by this mod for some Anomaly genes and the vanilla game to remove family generation for duplicates. (Req. human component and genes triggers options) 4) Fix for rare null ref that breaks UI if you own the Anomaly DLC but for some reason disabled it in your current playthrough.
Disabling this option will not break the mod, but some genes will work incorrectly or slower than expected. If you do not have other fixes for the above, it is strongly recommended to leave the option enabled.
Use this option only as a last resort and if you know what you are doing.
A restart is required.
LanguageData.WVC_ToolTip_enable_xagHumanComponent:
If enabled, the mod will add a special component to humans.
This component is responsible for a number of important things: 1) Some gene mechanics use it, such as the Duplicator, Undead, and some genes with timers. 2) This component checks genes for errors. It fixes incorrect overrides, removes null references, adds missing gene abilities, removes null gene traits, and resets the cache.
It should not affect performance, since the comp caches all the necessary information and requests it only when the player directly interacts with the pawn (like opening the inspect panel).
Use this option only as a last resort and if you know what you are doing.
A restart is required.
